Document:
#Glossary of Crimes
##Modifiers & Special Situations
Special modifiers that may change the sentencing for crimes committed depending on circumstance.
Modifier
Description
Notes
Clear and Present Danger
Crimes committed in the interest of saving life, property, or preventing a disaster may have their sentences reduced, voided, or expunged.
This modifier is primarily to cover situations such as breaking into an area one does not have access to, in the interest of saving someone's life or otherwise with good intent.
Repeat Offender
Repeat offenses warrant the charge be sentenced at one higher level of severity than normal. Further repeat offenses can be elevated again.
This applies to being re-arrested for committing the same, or similar, crimes as one was previously charged with. This can include being elevated all the way to permanent confinement.
Self Defense
Actions taken to defend ones self from an assailant may have sentences reduced, voided, or expunged if the officer(s) involved deem that use of force reasonable.
This isn't a school. You can punch back, but don't be out of scale.
Insanity
Those of unstable mind should be referred to medical treatment or deconversion. If deconverted or otherwise cured of their ailment, any charges against the suspect should be dropped.
This is intended to be applied to hypnosis, mind control, obsession, or otherwise actual incapacitation.[italic]'Insanity' is not an excuse for high crimes.[/italic]
Resisting Arrest
Knowingly resisting attempts at legal arrest by members of the security team. Screaming bloody murder over the radio counts as resisting. These individuals should have their sentences increased by 1-2 minutes.
Running will only increase your sentence.
Griseo Maris
Coordinated behavior by those with no clear assigned duty which interrupts the normal function of any one department warrant any crimes committed during such behavior to be sentenced at one higher level of severity than normal.
English: Grey Tide. Go be useful you [bolditalic]LOVELY PEOPLE.[/bolditalic]
Escaped Prisoner
Someone who has escaped their cell should be recaptured and returned to the brig. Their timer should be reset to the full length of the original sentence. Perma inmates who escape permabrig or attempt to while using force are eligible for Capital punishment.
Escaping will only delay the inevitable.
Cooperation
Being helpful to members of security, revealing things during questioning, or surrendering yourself to the brig of your own volition. Note that simply cooperating with a legal arrest does not apply this modifier. Cooperative suspects should have their sentences reduced by 1-2 minutes, or otherwise lowered in severity.
Come quietly.
##Infraction
All of these crimes are punished with a monetary fine ranging from 50 - 200 credits depending on severity.
Code
Crime
Description
Notes
003
Superficial Vandalism
To deface the station or property of another individual.
Minor damage that can be repaired with space cleaner or other cosmetic only damage.
008
Creating a Hazard
To create a situation which may lead to minor harm of others or impede normal department operations.
Minor unsafe behavior can also be charged under this crime.
011
Disorderly Conduct
To conduct ones self in a way unsuitable for the retention of one's position.
Minorly disruptive behavior that isn't otherwise illegal, such as drunkenly harassing a crewmember.
014
Disruptive Comms
To knowingly disrupt or needlessly populate a radio frequency with the intent to be a nuisance to users of that frequency.
Radio spam bad.\nThis can also include incessantly lying or making fake callouts over the radio. In addition to a fine, suspects may have their radio equipment confiscated.
##Mischief
All of these crimes carry a [bold]3 minute sentence[/bold], or a 300 credit fine.
Code
Crime
Description
Notes
101
Assault
To use physical force against someone without the apparent intent to injure them. Spitting on somebody or shoving them over is considered assault.
This covers minor criminal uses of force, without intent to seriously injure. That would be Aggravated Assault.
103
Vandalism
To damage, destroy, or permanently deface non-critical furniture, vendors, or personal property.
Large amounts of damage should be charged with Major Vandalism, damage to critical infrastructure may be a form of Sabotage.
105
Obstruction of Justice
To resist legal searches, detainment, or questioning by security.
This should be charged for crewmembers who resist legal detainment even if they have committed no other crimes. Otherwise, use the Resisting Arrest modifier. Security should note that wordlessly stunning someone for a search is [italic]not[/italic] legal detainment.
106
Vigilantism
To perform the responsibilities and duties of the security department without approval or due cause to act.
Validhunting bad. Let the redshirts do their job.
107
Pickpocketing
To steal items from another's person.
Small or easily replaceable items should be charged with this, otherwise consider a higher class of theft.
108
Negligence
To be negligent in one's duty to an extent that it may cause harm, illness, or other negative effect, to another.
This is the charge for the doctor that accidentally mixes bruizine with lacerinol, or a scientist who accidentally floods artifact science with room temperature CO2 or N2O.
109
Trespass
To be in an area which a person does not have access to, without due cause. This counts for general areas of the station, and trespass in restricted areas is a more serious crime.
This crime covers non forcible entry into areas, such as following someone through a door. You should ensure that someone wasn't allowed access legally before charging with this, as there are lots of legal reasons to be in an area someone does not have access to.
111
Fine Evasion
To purposefully avoid or refuse to pay a legal fine.
A reasonable time and notification should be given to the plaintiff.
114
Disturbing the Peace
To knowingly organize a movement which disrupts the normal operations of a department.
Establishing a peaceful strike or otherwise disrupting standard department order without attachment to criminal entities.
115
Discriminatory \nLanguage
To use language which demeans, generalizes, or otherwise de-personafies the individual at which it is targeted.
This charge mainly applies to speciesism. [bolditalic]Note that actual, OOC racism is not acceptable under the rules and should be ahelped.[/bolditalic]
##Misdemeanors
All of these crimes carry a [bold]5 minute[/bold] sentence.
Code
Crime
Description
Notes
201
Aggravated Assault
To take physical action against a person with intent to injure, but not to kill.
This covers criminal uses of force, with intent to injure, but no intent to kill. That would be some variation of Attempted Murder. If a deadly weapon was used, consider Assault with a Deadly Weapon instead.
202
Assault of an Officer
To use physical force against a Department Head or member of Security without the apparent intent to injure them.
Criminals who attempt to disarm or grab officers while fleeing are guilty of this, even if bare handed.
203
Major Vandalism
To destroy or damage large amounts of non-critical furniture, vendors, or personal property.
If the damage is severe enough that it actually causes harm or seriously impedes the work of the station crew, consider charging with Sabotage instead.
204
Animal Cruelty
To unnecessarily harm, torture, or kill animals.
Harming is justified if it is self-defence. Livestock and monkeys can be killed for scientific or culinary purposes. Pests such as rats and roaches can be killed freely.
205
Aiding and Abetting
To knowingly assist a criminal, including by interfering with a lawful arrest.
Knowingly assisting a criminal is a crime. This includes but is not limited to: Interfering with an arrest, leaking confidential information to suspects, breaking a prisoner out of the brig/prison, hiding a fugitive, or knowingly providing medical care without informing security. Exact knowledge of the original crime is not needed for this charge to apply. Freeing a permanently incarcerated prisoner or interfering with a lawful execution is a much more severe offense and can cause this crime to be elevated to a Grand Felony.
206
Contraband
To possess weapons, or equipment not approved for use aboard Nanotrasen stations. This includes equipment produced by The Syndicate, Wizard Federation, or any other hostile organization as defined within Code 305 and 505.
Reminder: Contraband equipment should be stored in evidence. It is not candy to hand out to Security. Particularly dangerous Contraband should be charged under 306 instead. The status if an item is contraband can be seen when inspecting it.
207
Petty Theft
To steal equipment or items from a workplace, or items of high value from one's person.
This includes things like insulated gloves, crew pinpointers, normal ID cards, or personal items.
208
High \nNegligence
To be negligent in one's duty to an extent that it may cause harm to multiple individuals, a department, or in a manor which directly leads to a serious injury of another person which requires emergency medical treatment.
Virologists releasing spanish flu on accident, or scientists that flood science/toxins with plasma get charged here. It can also include engineers who accidentally delaminate the SM or wander around with highly radioactive material.
209
Trespass, Restricted Areas
Trespassing in an area which may lead to the injury of self, or others. This included any Command area, any Security area, as well as Atmospherics, the Engine Room, and any other dangerous area.
This list is not exhaustive, but should not be extorted to increase sentencing.
210
Breaking and \nEntering
Forced entry to areas where the subject does not have access to. This counts for general areas, and breaking into restricted areas is a more serious crime.
Note that crewmembers can be charged with this even if they did not step foot into the area itself. Hacking open a door and running off is still a crime.\nThis crime should be charged instead of 109 Trespass, not stacked with it.
211
Insubordination
To knowingly disobey a lawful order from a superior.
This crime can normally be accompanied by a demotion at the discretion of the relevant head of staff.
212
Fraud
To misrepresent ones intention in the interest of gaining property or money from another individual.
Lying to someone about a cargo bounty, for example.
213
Drug \nPossession
The possession of illegal drugs for personal use.
Illegal drugs include any hallucinogenic, poisonous, or stimulant drug not produced for medicinal purposes. Drugs produced for research purposes and kept within Botany, Chemistry, or Science are exempt from this.
214
Rioting
To act as a member in a group which collectively commits acts of major vandalism, sabotage, breaking & entering, or other felony crimes.
A riot typically constitutes five or more people acting in a coordinated effort. The ringleaders should be charged with 314 instead.
##Felonies
These crimes carry a [bold]7 minute[/bold] brig sentence.
Code
Crime
Description
Notes
301
Manslaughter
To unintentionally kill someone through negligent, but not malicious, actions.
Intent is important. Accidental deaths caused by negligent actions, such as creating workplace hazards (e.g. gas leaks), tampering with equipment, medical malpractice, excessive force, and confinement in unsafe conditions are examples of Manslaughter.
302
Assault with a Deadly Weapon
To use physical force, through a deadly weapon, against someone without the apparent intent to kill them.
Any variety of tools, chemicals or even construction materials can inflict serious injury in short order. If the victim was brutalized beyond critical, consider charging them with Attempted Murder.
303
Sabotage
To hinder the work of the crew or station through malicious actions.
Deliberately releasing N₂O, bolting or electrifying doors, disabling the power network, constructing barricades, and destroying nonvital machinery are but some of many means of sabotage. For more violent forms, see Grand Sabotage.
304
Botanical Illicit Production
Making illegal botanical drugs, weapons, or invasive flora.
Producing drugs, weapons, or invasive flora such as kudzu or gatfruits. Botanists are permitted to make dangerous plants for research purposes so long as they stay within botany. If the invasive flora is released onto the station, charge with 504 Biological Terror instead.
305
Enemy of the \nCorporation
To knowingly act as, or aid, a recruiter, representative, messenger, ally, benefactor, or other associate of an enemy of Nanotrasen.
Any syndicate corporation, non-authorized religious practitioner (heretic), or bloodsucker clan is considered an enemy of Nanotrasen. Having ties to these groups while having committed no other actual crimes should be charged under this.Note that in addition to brig time, EOTCs should if possible be fitted with a tracking implant and taken alive to Central Command for interrogation at end of shift.If the station alert level is at Red, or otherwise undergoing a severe emergency, security may at their discretion charge this crime as a Grand Felony instead if they deem an EOTC too great a risk to release.
306
Dangerous Contraband
To possess restricted or illegal equipment which has a primary purpose of causing harm to others, or large amounts of destruction.
Energy swords, explosives, guns (without a weapons permit), and so on count here. And no, the bartender can not have a captured LMG because they have a weapon permit. Again, security, those are not toys, put them in evidence. Scientists and Miners are permitted to possess explosives within their respective departments.
307
Theft
To steal rare, expensive (over 1000 credit value), or restricted equipment from secure areas or one's person.
This includes the theft of weapons, rare equipment, or ID cards which could lead into high security areas, such as the Head of Personnel's, or Head of Security's. Cargo breaking open crates to illegally arm and armor themselves are also guilty of Theft.
309
Trespass, Max Security
Trespassing in any of the following without appropriate permission or access: The Armory, the Vault, the AI satellite, or AI upload.
Breaking into the Armory specifically is an extremely serious crime and can be elevated to a Grand Felony if security believes the intent was malicious.
310
Breaking and \nEntering, Restricted
To forcibly break into any restricted area as defined in Code 209.
This should be charged instead of 209 Restricted Trespass, not stacked with it.
311
Dereliction of Duty
To willfully abandon an obligation that is critical to the station's continued operation.
Like Insubordination, a demotion is often included in the sentence. Emphasis on the word critical: An officer taking a break is not dereliction by itself. An officer taking a break knowing that operatives are shooting up the Captain is. Engineers who do not secure a power source at the start of the shift and heads of staff who abandon the station can also be charged.
312
Identity Theft
To assume the identity of another individual.
This can include utilizing someone else's PDA in order to impersonate them.
313
Drug \nDistribution
The possession of dangerous or illegal drugs in a quantity greater than that which is reasonable for personal consumption.
You should probably consult your boss before charging someone with this.
314
Inciting a Riot
To perform actions in the interest of causing large amounts of unrest up to and including rioting.
This is for the ring leader in any insurrection or riot. Prisoners may have their radios removed and be outfitted with tracking implants. In addition, it is highly advisable to contain them in areas which are not visible to the general populace.
315
Kidnapping
To kidnap a person or hold them hostage.
Holding anyone somewhere against their will without legitimate reason counts for this. Unlawful arrests are considered kidnapping.
##Grand Felonies
These crimes can result in [bold]Execution, Permanent Prison Time[/bold].
Criminals can be sentenced to [bold]Execution[/bold] or [bold]Forced Cyborgization[/bold] if it would not be reasonably feasible to contain them in the Prison Wing. \nThis can include the possession of abilities allowing teleportation/other exit from the prison wing trivially, innate knowledge of dangerous martial arts, or critical station emergencies preventing timely permabrigging.
\nOnly the [bold][color=red]Captain, HoS, and Warden[/bold][/color] can authorize a [bold]Permanent Sentence[/bold]. Only the [bold][color=red]Captain and the Head of Security[/bold][/color] can authorize an [bold]Execution or Forced Cyborgization[/bold].
Code
Crime
Description
Notes
401
Murder
To purposefully kill someone.
Punishment should fit the nature of both the crime and the criminal. Murder committed by temporary emotional distress, such as fear or anger, can be argued to warrant lower punishments. Murder committed in the interest of defending life or departmental/personal property can have the sentence reduced or voided. If the victim willingly succumbs while in critical condition, it is not Murder. Duels to the death or rage cages are not Murder, so long as all parties fully consent. [bold]Unauthorized vigilante field executions are classed as Murder.[/bold]
402
Attempted Murder
To use physical force against a person with the apparent intent to kill them.
Intent can be difficult to prove. Normally this charge is applied when the victim is brutalized beyond critical, but remember, if a person attempts to render first aid after the victim falls into a critical state they may not have intended to kill them. [bold]When in doubt, a lesser assault charge should be applied.[/bold] The usage of expressly lethal weapons such as firearms or energy swords may be considered proof of intent.
403
Grand Sabo\ntage
To destroy or modify station assets or equipment without which the station may collapse or otherwise become uninhabitable.
This may include purging of station records, bombing, arson, spacing large areas of the station, purposefully delaminating the SM, destroying the SMES power storage equipment, plasma flooding, or unauthorized tampering with the station’s AI.
405
De-Implantation
To knowingly remove legally placed security implants, or otherwise violate a legal parole agreement.
This should be charged both to the parolee and the surgeon who removed the implants. Surgeons who were not aware the implants were placed by security, but failed to check, may be charged with High Negligence and given a timed sentence instead.
407
Grand Theft
To steal items of high value or sensitive nature.
Syndicate agents frequently attempt to steal cutting-edge technology or corporate secrets. Examples include: Unique items, Head of Department specific Equipment, or the Captain's ID. This is by no means an exhaustive list of items that are high value to the syndicate. When in doubt, use common sense: certain items that are stolen that can cause massive problems throughout the station. Remember if something is locked up in a secure area it probably should not be taken without prior permission.
415
Brainwashing
To brainwash or enslave people against their will.
Brainwashing surgery, heretic ghouls, hypnosis, and illegitimate forced cyborgification fall here. The brainwasher is additionally held accountable for every crime a brainwashed person has committed.
##Capital Crimes
These crimes should result in [bold]Execution, Permanent Prison Time[/bold].
\nOnly the [bold][color=red]Captain, HoS, and Warden[/bold][/color] can authorize a [bold]Permanent Sentence[/bold]. Only the [bold][color=red]Captain and the Head of Security[/bold][/color] can authorize an [bold]Execution or Forced Cyborgization[/bold].
Code
Crime
Description
Notes
501
Mass Murder
To intentionally kill three or more individuals.
Criminals in the process of on-going murder sprees may be assumed Armed and Dangerous, and detained with lethal force.
502
Prime Murder
To commit the act of Murder, with clear intent to kill and prevent the revival of the victim.
This includes debraining, intentionally disemboweling, destroying, spacing, or hiding a corpse.
504
Biological Terror
To knowingly release, cause, or otherwise cause the station to become affected by a disease, plant, or other biological form which may spread uncontained and/or cause serious physical harm.
Don't immediately lynch the Virologist, but an investigation might be a good idea. Can also apply to Botanists who release invasive flora aboard the station.
505
Priority Threat
To be a high priority threat to the station.
To be a member of, or knowingly aid, any organization or entity defined as a Priority Threat within the relevant section in Space Law. Deconversion should take priority over other sentencing if possible.
514
Mutiny
To act individually, or as a group, to overthrow or subvert the established Chain of Command without lawful and legitimate cause
Mutiny is not as clear cut as it may seem, there may be a legitimate reason for their actions, such as their head of staff being utterly incompetent. This is one of the few crimes where it is recommended to always seek a third party opinion. If their actions are determined to be for the betterment of Nanotrasen, consider a timed sentence or even a full pardon.
515
Genetic Mutilation
To purposefully modify an individual's genetic code without consent, or with intent to harm.
Purposefully irradiating or transforming people falls under this law.
[bold][color=green]Glory to Nanotrasen[/bold][/color]
